context_pointers.vague_reference: emit true in the closing action
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The condition indicating the system should close the open form tag by generating '</form>'.

context_pointers.vague_reference: prepend is list with options object
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This refers to a specific render path where 'prepend' contains an object alongside other values, applying any classes present in that object to the input wrapper.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_basic_controls.json.description: Render a single labelled form control by binding the helper to an entity description and asking for one of its fields. The control type is inferred from the field's declared data type, and the rendered group wraps a label and an input together. When the bound field is marked required by the entity metadata, the input additionally carries native required/validity wiring and an accessibility flag, and the group exposes a required marker class. Output is the generated HTML for the control group.

feature2_1_addon_text.json.description: Wrap a labelled text control in an input group with leading (prepend) or trailing (append) static text add-ons. A single string yields one add-on segment; a list of strings yields several segments in order. The input sits inside an input-group container together with the add-on segments. Output is the generated HTML for the control group.

cases.expected_output: @
@

feature2_2_addon_button.json.description: Wrap a labelled text control in an input group whose add-on is itself a rendered button (or several buttons) placed before or after the input. The button markup is produced by the same helper's button builder and embedded directly into the input group. Output is the generated HTML for the control group.

feature2_3_addon_options.json.description: Attach add-ons to a labelled control where the final element of the add-on list is an options map applied to the surrounding input-group container. A size option maps to a sizing modifier class on the group, and arbitrary class/attribute entries are merged onto the group element. Both leading and trailing placement are supported. Output is the generated HTML for the control group.

cases.expected_output:
@
@

feature2_4_addon_error.json.description: Render a control with an add-on while the bound field carries a validation error. The input-group container and the control group both gain an invalid-state class, the input is marked invalid and linked to a separate feedback block by id, and the feedback block carries the error message. Both leading and trailing add-on placements are exercised. Output is the generated HTML for the control group.

cases.expected_output:
@
error message
@
error message

feature2_5_addon_basic_widgets.json.description: Apply a leading add-on directly to standalone form widgets (single-line text, multi-line text, a select box, a file picker, and a date-time picker) rendered outside the labelled-control wrapper. Each widget is placed inside an input-group container next to the add-on segment, and each widget keeps its own appropriate styling class. Output is the generated HTML for each widget in turn.

cases.expected_output:
@
@
@
@
@

feature3_1_form_open_close.json.description: Open a form bound to an entity and close it. Opening emits the form start tag with method, charset, a role attribute and the resolved action URL. Closing emits the matching form end tag. Output is the generated markup.

cases.expected_output: <form accept-charset="utf-8" action="/articles/add" method="post" role="form">
</form>

feature3_2_form_alignment.json.description: Open a form in a chosen layout alignment. An inline alignment adds inline layout/grid classes to the form tag, and an optional spacing override replaces the default gutter class. A horizontal alignment adds a horizontal layout class. Output is the generated form start tag.

cases.expected_output: <form accept-charset="utf-8" action="/articles/add" class="form-inline row g-3 align-items-center" method="post" role="form">
<form accept-charset="utf-8" action="/articles/add" class="form-horizontal" method="post" role="form">

feature3_3_invalid_alignment.json.description: Opening a form with an unsupported alignment value is rejected. The adapter surfaces a neutral error category identifying the offending alignment option rather than producing markup.

cases.expected_output: error=invalid_alignment

feature3_4_custom_grid.json.description: Open a form with an explicit two-column grid specification (label column width and input column width). The specification may be given either as a positional pair or as an indexed map keyed by column position. A control rendered afterwards lays the label and the input wrapper out across the requested grid widths. Output is the generated HTML for the control group.

feature3_5_templates_file.json.description: Open a form that loads an external template set by name, overriding the default markup templates. A control rendered afterwards uses the custom container template, which contributes an extra wrapper class around the control group. Output is the generated HTML for the control group.

feature3_6_horizontal_from_config.json.description: Configure the helper up front with a horizontal alignment plus a custom template fragment for the checkbox group, then open the form and render controls. A text control is laid out across the default horizontal grid; a boolean control uses the custom checkbox group fragment and is offset to align with the input column. Output is the form start tag followed by the generated HTML for each control.

feature4_1_buttons.json.description: Render a standalone button. By default it is a submit-type button with a base and a secondary variant class. A style keyword supplied as a class is translated into the corresponding themed variant class while the base class is preserved. Output is the generated button markup.

cases.expected_output: <button class="btn btn-secondary" type="submit">Submit</button>
<button class="btn-success btn" type="submit">Submit</button>

feature4_2_submit.json.description: Render a submit input wrapped in a container. Classes supplied either as a space-separated string or as a list are merged, and a default secondary variant class is appended. Output is the generated submit markup.

feature5_1_tooltip_disabled_label.json.description: Render a control whose label is suppressed while a tooltip helper text is requested. With the label disabled, the control group contains only the input (carrying its required/validity wiring) and no label element. Output is the generated HTML for the control group.

feature5_2_widget_class_injection.json.description: Render assorted standalone widgets directly and confirm each receives the appropriate styling class: single-line text, select, multi-line text, date-time, file, checkbox, radio group, and color all get the styling class that matches their kind, and a user-supplied class is preserved alongside the injected one. Output is the generated markup for each widget in turn.

feature6_1_feedback_style.json.description: Control how validation feedback is presented for an errored field. A tooltip feedback style renders the feedback block as a floating tooltip and adds a positioning class to the control group; the default style renders the feedback as an inline block. The feedback style may be set as a helper-wide default and overridden per control, and it composes with the form alignment (default, inline, horizontal). The input is always marked invalid and linked to the feedback block by id. Output is the generated HTML for the control group.

cases.expected_output: error message
error message

feature6_2_form_group_position.json.description: When a tooltip feedback style is active, the control group's CSS positioning context can be selected. A helper-wide positioning setting applies a corresponding positioning class to the control group, and it can be overridden per control. Output is the generated HTML for the control group.

cases.expected_output: error message
error message

feature7_1_aria_attributes.json.description: Manage accessibility attributes on a control. A hidden field never receives accessibility attributes. For a visible errored/required field, the accessibility flags (required, invalid, described-by linkage) are emitted by default but each can be individually disabled or overridden with a custom value, and disabling one leaves the others intact. Output is the generated HTML for the control.

cases.expected_output: <input class="is-invalid" id="title" name="title" type="hidden">
error message

feature8_1_spacing_override.json.description: Choose the spacing (margin) utility class applied to a control group. The spacing can be set as a helper-wide default, supplied when opening the form, or supplied per control, with the more specific scope winning over the broader one. Spacing supplied at form-open scope is reset once the form is closed so a subsequently opened form falls back to the default. Output is the generated HTML for the affected control group(s).

feature8_2_spacing_disable.json.description: Disabling spacing removes the margin utility class from a control group entirely. Disabling can be requested as a helper-wide default, when opening the form, or per control, again with the more specific scope taking precedence. Output is the generated HTML for the affected control group(s).

feature9_1_floating_labels.json.description: Render a control with a floating label. The label is requested in floating mode, which places the input before the label inside a floating-label wrapper and derives a placeholder attribute on the input from the label text. The placeholder text is humanized from the field name, including for dotted/nested field paths and for fields whose name ends in an id suffix. Output is the generated HTML for the control group.

_constraints.input_condition: Any JSON input provided to the adapter
_constraints.output_rule: Renderer must never crash or output invalid HTML

_constraints.input_condition: Every generated HTML tag
_constraints.output_rule: Attributes within start tags must be sorted alphabetically by attribute name

_constraints.input_condition: Any string literal passed into rendered HTML
_constraints.output_rule: HTML special characters must be entity-escaped

_constraints.input_condition: action: control includes options: [prepend|append] provided as a string
_constraints.output_rule: Render a 'span' with class 'input-group-text' containing the string

_constraints.input_condition: action: control includes options: [prepend|append] provided as a list of strings
_constraints.output_rule: Render multiple 'span' elements with class 'input-group-text' in order

_constraints.input_condition: action: control includes options: [prepend|append] provided as a list where last element is an object
_constraints.output_rule: Merge the object's 'size' property into input-group classes (e.g., 'input-group-lg') and any other keys (like 'class') into element attributes

_constraints.input_condition: options: prepend OR append
_constraints.output_rule: Text or Button add-ons appear BEFORE the input when 'prepend' is set

_constraints.input_condition: options: prepend OR append
_constraints.output_rule: Text or Button add-ons appear AFTER the input when 'append' is set

_constraints.input_condition: Entity metadata marks field as required OR 'required' operation is set to true
_constraints.output_rule: Apply specific attribute sets to the rendered input
exact_values: data-validity-message="This field cannot be left empty"

_constraints.input_condition: Entity metadata marks field as required OR 'required' operation is set to true, AND action: control is used
_constraints.output_rule: Add 'required' class to wrapper div AND to the label div

_constraints.input_condition: Entity metadata declares errors for the field OR '_' flag is set
_constraints.output_rule: Apply error classes to all rendering layers

_constraints.input_condition: Options include spread merging (..., ...)
_constraints.output_rule: Merge extras into control classes

_constraints.input_condition: Action is control AND the entity carries a validation error message
_constraints.output_rule: Render a separate div with 'invalid-feedback' class IDENTICAL to the HTML of the control group + add on

_constraints.input_condition: Action is one of: text, textarea, select, file, dateTime
_constraints.output_rule: Render appropriate base widget with inferred type

_constraints.input_condition: Action is button
_constraints.output_rule: Render button with base class 'btn btn-secondary', optional 'class' override

_constraints.input_condition: Action is submit
_constraints.output_rule: Wrap input in 'submit' div, apply merged classes, append 'btn-secondary'

_constraints.input_condition: Action is create AND options: templates provided
_constraints.output_rule: Apply custom templates to control wrapper

_constraints.input_condition: action: create AND options: templateSet provided
_constraints.output_rule: Apply custom wrapper templates

_constraints.input_condition: Action is create AND options: align provided (map, list, or validated string)
_constraints.output_rule: Apply Bootstrap alignment class to form tag (e.g., 'row', 'form-horizontal')

_constraints.input_condition: Action is create AND options: align is unsupported value
_constraints.output_rule: Output error line

_constraints.input_condition: Action is control does NOT include options: align, AND action: create did NOT specify grid
_constraints.output_rule: Embed label and input wrapper inside a div with 'row' class defaulting to md-3 + md-9

_constraints.input_condition: Action is control DOES align
_constraints.output_rule: Embed label and input wrapper inside a div with alignment classes, balanced widths

_constraints.input_condition: action: create WITH '8' as first map key OR list of 8 aligned
_constraints.output_rule: Apply 8-column width to first label column

_constraints.input_condition: Action is end with emit true
_constraints.output_rule: Generate closing form tag

_constraints.input_condition: Widget selector is 'text' AND options.prepend is list with options object
_constraints.output_rule: Input wrapper includes options object classes

_constraints.input_condition: All handling
_constraints.output_rule: Field name is derived from JSON schema key (e.g. 'schema.password' -> 'Password') or operation action (e.g. 'TEXT' -> 'Title')
